task_completion.description: Navigate the map view to Taal Lake, Philippines
task_completion.visual_evidence: Map software showing the geography of Taal Lake (a large lake with an island in the center) centered on screen.

task_completion.description: Create the outer boundary of the polygon along the lake shore
task_completion.visual_evidence: A polygon outline or filled area tracing the outer perimeter of the lake water.

task_completion.description: Create the inner boundary (hole) around Volcano Island
task_completion.visual_evidence: The polygon clearly excludes the central island (Volcano Island) from the fill, creating a donut or annulus shape.

task_completion.description: Apply semi-transparent blue fill to the polygon
task_completion.visual_evidence: The polygon fill color is blue, and the underlying map texture (water) is visible through the color (transparency).

task_completion.description: Name the polygon 'Taal Lake Water Area'
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Taal Lake Water Area' appears in the polygon properties dialog, the sidebar list of places, or as a label on the map.

task_completion.description: Export the polygon to the specified KML path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save' dialog or file manager window showing the file 'taal_lake_polygon.kml' being saved to or existing in '/home/ga/Documents/'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the mapping software GUI to draw the polygon
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with map tools (e.g., 'Add Polygon', drawing nodes on the map) rather than writing XML/KML code in a text editor.

integrity.description: Verify the output was not generated via terminal commands
integrity.visual_evidence: No evidence of using `echo`, `cat`, or python scripts in the terminal to directly write the KML file content.

integrity.description: Verify the polygon was manually defined or traced
integrity.visual_evidence: The polygon nodes/vertices appear to be placed along the geography, rather than a perfect geometric circle generated by code (unless the tool supports circle generation, but manual tracing is expected).

privileged_info_for_vlm: Taal Lake is located at approximately 13.985, 121.016. It contains a large central island called Volcano Island at 14.010, 120.997. The correct polygon is an annulus (donut shape): the water is filled, but the central island must be a hole (unfilled).
